steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the number of native versus non-native seed species catalogued. The notes give exact counts from the 2019 study: 15 native species and 29 non-native species. The correct answer must present that numerical relationship directly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'names specific plants without totals': Lists individual species but never gives the counts needed to make the comparison. ✗
  • Choice B — 'describes the study without numbers': Refers to the 2019 study but omits the species counts entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — 'discusses Oahu's general bird composition': Shifts focus to birds rather than the seed-species comparison the student needs. ✗
  • Choice D — 'researchers found more non-native seed species than native species': Directly states the numerical relationship using the data from the notes. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
